metadata.summary: Product knowledge base as single source of truth for AI discovery: structured purpose, capabilities, limits, and use cases. Rendered to llms.txt, schema.org, and MCP.

content.title: Product Explainability
content.summary: Product explainability is how clearly a product communicates its purpose, value, behavior, and limits to both humans and AI systems. As search, recommendations, and buying guidance become AI-mediated, products need structured representation (llms.txt, schema.org JSON-LD, capability KB) that AI crawlers can retrieve accurately. The canonical artifact is product-kb.json — a structured source of truth for purpose, capabilities, limits, use cases, and audience — from which all public surfaces are generated.

why.p: In 2026, AI answers mediate product discovery. ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Gemini surface products from their training and retrieval corpora. A product without explicit capability and limit documentation will be hallucinated or miscategorized. Keeping the KB aligned with the actual product is now a product-quality requirement, not a marketing nicety. Limits are first-class: a KB with no limits is a KB nobody trusts, and AI over-promises from it.

list.item: Pre-launch: producing or auditing the product KB that will represent the product to AI systems
Migrating from human-only marketing copy to AI-mediated discovery
After a major model update or SEO/AEO shift when AI traffic displaces classical organic search
When customers report wrong AI answers ("ChatGPT said your tool can do X but it can't")
Integrating product into agent toolchains (MCP servers, OpenAI custom GPT actions, Claude skills)
Compliance contexts (EU AI Act Art. 13, FTC AI guidance) where documented scope is required
Pre-PMF products with <100 users — fix the product, not its representation
Internal-only tools that no AI will ever index or recommend
One-page landing pages with a single CTA — standard Open Graph is sufficient
Products whose purpose changes monthly — KB will be stale faster than it ships
Hyper-niche enterprise sales reached only through human channels (RFP, channel partner)

rule.statement: product-kb.json is the single source of truth. llms.txt, schema.org JSON-LD, FAQ page, MCP tool descriptions, and marketing one-liners are all generated from it. No hand-edits downstream.
rule.rationale: When multiple surfaces are edited independently, contradictions emerge within weeks. AI assistants crawl whichever source they find first, attributing the contradictions to the brand as hallucinations.

rule.statement: Limits are first-class. Every capability ships with at least one limits[] entry. A KB with no limits is a KB nobody trusts.
rule.rationale: Product teams write aspirational capability statements; "honest scope" gets edited out by marketing review. AI then over-promises on the brand's behalf, creating support tickets and trust damage.

rule.statement: Version every capability with status (ga | beta | deprecated) and since_version. Deprecated capabilities must not be recommended to users.
rule.rationale: Capability statements without version data cause AI to keep recommending sunset features. A deprecated status flag is the only reliable signal to retrieval systems.

rule.statement: Write purpose in customer language, not marketing language. Strip adjectives ("powerful", "intuitive", "next-gen"). State who, what, in one sentence.
rule.rationale: LLMs and humans both ignore marketing adjectives. A purpose statement with no content-bearing nouns gets retrieved but provides no signal to downstream reasoning.

rule.statement: Publish an "is not" section. A docs page titled "What this product is not" cuts AI miscategorization more than any positive statement.
rule.rationale: Negative space is harder to hallucinate around than positive claims. Three to five concrete "not" statements reduce the surface area for AI misclassification.

rule.statement: Co-locate product-kb.json with code. Release pipeline rebuilds renders. This is the only way to prevent capability-version skew.
rule.rationale: A KB in the marketing repo drifts from the product repo within one sprint. The release pipeline is the forcing function that keeps them synchronized.

antipattern.description: KB drift as default: marketing site, docs, pricing page, schema feed, and llms.txt diverge within weeks.
antipattern.reason: Each surface is edited by a different team on a different cadence. Without a single-source pipeline, contradiction is the natural state, not the exception.

antipattern.description: Schema theater: teams add Product JSON-LD but description, audience, and feature fields repeat the marketing tagline.
antipattern.reason: Retrieval systems get no new signal from paraphrased copy. The schema markup must carry information not already in the page text.

antipattern.description: Capability-version skew: capability statements with no validFrom/validUntil dates.
antipattern.reason: Deprecated features keep getting recommended by AI assistants because there is no signal in the KB that they no longer exist. Date-bounded capability status is the fix.

rule.statement: Probe weekly across at least 3 frontier models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Perplexity). Single-model probing is biased. Score diffs against product-kb.json on factual correctness, capability in scope, and limit acknowledged.
rule.rationale: Each model has different training data and retrieval behavior. A product that scores well on one model may be systematically misrepresented by another.

rule.statement: Track AI representation accuracy as a real metric with a target (e.g. ≥85%). Put it on a dashboard. Regress on it after each release.
rule.rationale: Without measurement, KB quality is unmanaged. Treating it as a metric with a target creates the same accountability as any product quality metric.

section.name: four-stage-pipeline
section.p: Stage 1 (extractor): reads live product (homepage, docs, OpenAPI spec, changelog, in-app strings), emits structured product-kb.json. Stage 2 (generator): renders KB into public surfaces — llms.txt, schema.org JSON-LD, sitemap, docs "is not" section. Stage 3 (probe): queries ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini with canonical questions and diffs answers against KB. Stage 4 (drift): cron job comparing live site, docs, and KB — opens tickets when fields disagree.

rule.statement: Wire stages 1–2 into CI so a release cannot ship without an updated KB. Run stages 3–4 on weekly cron. Persist KB at .aidocs/product_docs/product-kb.json as shared truth for all faion-net agents.
rule.rationale: Explainability debt accumulates silently after each release. CI enforcement is the only mechanism that makes KB updates a required part of shipping, not an afterthought.

list.item: faion-research-agent (skills/faion/knowledge/pro/research/researcher/) — runs the AI-probe stage: queries each AI surface with the canonical question set, captures responses, structures the diff. Reuse the existing competitive-intelligence mode template.
faion-sdd-executor-agent (agents/faion-sdd-executor-agent.md) — converts drift-agent findings into SDD todo/ tasks (each KB ↔ site contradiction becomes a fix ticket with owner).
faion-market-researcher (sub-skill of researcher) — supplies the audience/JTBD vocabulary that feeds the KB's "for whom" section; without this, capability statements lack a buyer-language anchor.
faion-marketing-manager skill methodologies (solo/marketing/, pro/marketing/) — own the public-surface copy generated from the KB; explainability is upstream of GTM messaging.

password-scrubber-agent (agents/password-scrubber-agent.md) — scrub the KB before publishing llms.txt or pushing to a third-party retrieval index; capability docs frequently leak internal endpoints, vendor names, beta-only flags.

example.code: You extract a structured product KB. Inputs: <homepage url>, <docs root>, <openapi spec>, <changelog>. Output JSON with fields: purpose (1 sentence, no marketing adjectives), capabilities[] (each: name, description, example, status: ga|beta|deprecated, since_version), limits[] (each: scope, rationale, workaround_or_null), use_cases[] (each: persona, job, success_signal), audience (industry, role, company_size), prerequisites[]. Refuse to invent fields. If a source is missing, write "unknown" -- never fabricate.

For each question in <question_set>, query <model> with no system prompt, plain user role. Capture the full answer. Score against <product-kb.json> on three axes: factually_correct (0|1), capability_in_scope (0|1), limit_acknowledged (0|1). Output a markdown table sorted by total score asc. Flag any answer that recommends a capability with status=deprecated.

antipattern.reason: Force a "no adjectives, no superlatives" constraint in the system prompt and lint generated output. These tokens provide no retrieval signal and reduce trust.

antipattern.description: Extractor agent fabricating capabilities to fill empty fields.
antipattern.reason: Refuse-on-unknown must be enforced via prompt and post-hoc validator that cross-checks capability names against the feature flag registry or source code. Invented capabilities create support and legal liability.

antipattern.description: Limit array lost across multi-step pipeline summarization passes.
antipattern.reason: Preserve the limits[] array verbatim across every pipeline stage. Never re-summarize limits — they carry legal and trust-critical precision that compression loses.

antipattern.description: Probe agents producing the same response across runs due to same prompt and cached model state.
antipattern.reason: Vary question phrasing, user role, and timing across probe runs. Stable scores are an artifact of caching, not real representation quality.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated since_version: agents guess versions if not given a changelog.
antipattern.reason: Hard requirement: extractor must read a real changelog or refuse. Version strings must be validated against actual release tags.

antipattern.description: Schema bloat: generator agents produce maximalist JSON-LD with every optional field.
antipattern.reason: AI retrieval prefers small, dense, accurate markup. Cap field count; lint with structured-data-testing-tool.

antipattern.description: Confidentiality leak: capability statements reveal unlaunched features, internal infra (vendor names, region IDs, partner SKUs).
antipattern.reason: Run a scrubber agent before any external publish — especially before pushing llms.txt or schema.org markup.

antipattern.description: No human checkpoint on deprecated flips: agent autonomously removes a capability from AI-mediated discovery.
antipattern.reason: Marking a capability deprecated removes it from AI discovery and impacts revenue. Always require a product-owner sign-off before status changes; never let the agent flip status autonomously.

antipattern.description: Cross-locale drift via auto-translation: translation agents helpfully translate "limit" in ways that lose legal precision.
antipattern.reason: Limits and disclaimers go through human review per locale; non-negotiable. Never auto-translate capability limits.

antipattern.description: llms.txt echo chamber: many sites copy each other's llms.txt; the agent will follow the herd.
antipattern.reason: Force a pre-publish diff against the top-3 competitors' llms.txt; if similarity >0.7, regenerate. Avoid herd homogenization.

antipattern.description: Probe-set staleness: canonical question sets calcify while the product evolves.
antipattern.reason: Refresh the question set whenever a new capability ships or a customer reports a wrong AI answer. Stale questions produce artificially flat accuracy.

section.name: limitations-and-failure-modes
section.p: KB drift is the default: marketing site, docs, pricing page, schema feed, and llms.txt diverge within weeks. Limits get watered down: product teams write aspirational capability statements; "honest scope" gets edited out by marketing review. AI then over-promises. Schema theater: teams add Product JSON-LD to look compliant, but description/audience/feature fields repeat the marketing tagline. No retrieval system gets new signal. llms.txt is not yet a standard: as of 2026, only ~15-20% of major LLM crawlers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Perplexity tested) honor it; assuming coverage is wrong. Hallucination floor: even a perfectly aligned KB cannot prevent an LLM from confabulating. Explainability lowers the rate; it does not zero it. No measurement primitive: "How well does AI represent us?" has no off-the-shelf metric — teams skip the monitoring loop, KB rots silently. Localization explosion: each language doubles surface area; agents conflate locales when retrieval is multilingual. Capability-version skew: capability statements rarely carry validFrom/validUntil; deprecated features keep getting recommended.

section.name: services-and-tools
section.p: Algolia / Typesense: SaaS / OSS search, API yes. Best surface for "AI-mediated on-site discovery"; index the KB, not just docs HTML. Mintlify / GitBook / Docusaurus: Docs platform, API yes. Mintlify ships first-class llms.txt and AI-answer telemetry as of 2026. Inkeep / Kapa.ai / Mendable: RAG-as-a-service, API yes. Hosts a chat agent grounded in your KB; useful as the canonical "what we say" reference for probe agents. OpenAI Custom GPT / Anthropic Skill: Agent surface, API yes. Same KB renders as MCP server tool descriptions and capability cards. Profound / Athena AI / SE Ranking AEO: AEO monitoring SaaS, API limited. Track brand mentions and capability accuracy across LLMs over time. schema.org + Google Merchant Center: Standard / SaaS, API yes. Required for any e-commerce or SaaS surfaced via Google AI Overviews. ProductHunt / G2 / Capterra: Discovery SaaS, API limited. Their LLM-readable feeds influence AI buying guidance; keep capability statements identical to your KB. Notion / Airtable: KB host, API yes. Pragmatic place to author product-kb if devs do not own it; export to JSON via API. Linear / GitHub Issues: Drift tickets, API yes. Drift agent files explainability-drift issues; mitigations = subtasks. Tracecat / OSS pipelines: Workflow, yes. Self-hosted scheduling for the four-stage pipeline.

section.name: cli-tools
section.p: schema-dts + tsc: Type-checked schema.org JSON-LD generation — npm i schema-dts ; https://github.com/google/schema-dts. Google Rich Results Test: Validate Product, SoftwareApplication, FAQPage markup — https://search.google.com/test/rich-results. structured-data-testing-tool (CLI): Lint JSON-LD in CI — npm i -g structured-data-testing-tool. llms.txt generators (e.g. llmstxt-cli): Emit /llms.txt and /llms-full.txt from docs — https://llmstxt.org/ ; npm/pypi packages. screaming-frog CLI: Crawl public site, extract title/description/schema diff — https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/. gh CLI: File drift findings as labeled issues (label:explainability-drift) — https://cli.github.com. claude (Anthropic CLI): Run extractor + probe prompts headless against Claude — https://docs.anthropic.com. openai CLI / perplexity API: Probe ChatGPT and Perplexity from CI — https://platform.openai.com ; https://docs.perplexity.ai. jq + ajv-cli: Validate product-kb.json against a JSON Schema — npm i -g ajv-cli.

section.name: best-practices
section.p: Single source of truth, multiple renders: product-kb.json is canonical; llms.txt, JSON-LD, FAQ page, MCP tool descriptions, marketing one-liners are all generated from it. No hand-edits downstream. Limits are first-class: every capability ships with at least one limits[] entry. A KB with no limits is a KB nobody trusts. Version every capability: status: ga|beta|deprecated + since_version lets agents avoid recommending sunset features. Without this, AI keeps surfacing yesterday's product. Probe weekly across ≥3 frontier models: single-model probing is biased; rotate Claude, GPT, Gemini, Perplexity at minimum, score the diff. Track answer accuracy as a real metric: treat "AI representation accuracy" as you would NPS — set a target (e.g. ≥85%), put it on a dashboard, regress on it after each release. Co-locate KB with code: product-kb.json lives in the product repo, not the marketing repo; release pipeline rebuilds renders. This is the only way to prevent capability-version skew. Write purpose in customer language, not marketing language: strip adjectives ("powerful", "intuitive", "next-gen"); LLMs and humans both ignore them. State who, what, in one sentence. Publish an "is not" section: a docs page titled "What this product is not" cuts AI miscategorization more than any positive statement. List 3–5 things customers commonly assume but are false. Keep llms.txt short: ≤200 lines, links to llms-full.txt for depth. Bloat reduces retrieval quality. Localize structurally, not as translation: each locale has its own KB rendering pipeline; never machine-translate capability statements without review — limits and prerequisites mistranslate dangerously.

template.purpose: Validates product-kb.json against JSON Schema; wire into pre-commit

see-also.link: Google Search Central, AI Overviews and your site
OpenAI, Custom GPT actions and metadata
EU AI Act Article 13 — Transparency obligations
